title: US claimed Iran nuclear 'decimated' -- but satellites show survivors -- 2026-05-09
narrative: In a press briefing on May 9 2026, a senior State Department official claimed that US military strikes conducted in the preceding 72 hours had 'decimated Iran's nuclear program,' destroying 'greater than 85 percent of active enrichment capacity' at Natanz, Fordow, and the Atomic Energy Organization of Iran facilities in Esfahan and Tehran. The official stated the strikes were 'surgical, proportionate, and fully consistent with international humanitarian law,' and that post-strike assessment via 'multiple independent intelligence streams' confirmed the program had been set back by a minimum of five years.
telemetry: COMMERCIAL SAR: ICEYE XSAR stripmap mode, 10-May-2026 02:30 UTC — Natanz main enrichment hall roof intact; no visible structural damage to process building; cooling tower rows A and B operating at normal thermal signature

VISIBLE BAND: Maxar WorldView-4, 10-May-2026 09:15 UTC — 11 of 14 declared centrifuge cascade halls at Natanz visibly undisturbed; temporary tent structures erected south of main hall consistent with ongoing manual operations, not decommissioning

THERMAL: Sentinel-3 SLSTR nighttime thermal, 09-May-2026 23:00 UTC — Esfahan conversion facility thermal footprint 94% of pre-strike baseline, inconsistent with destruction of 'greater than 85%' of processing capacity

SEISMIC: CTBTO International Data Centre — no infrasound or seismic signal consistent with large conventional detonation recorded at Natanz, Fordow, or Esfahan coordinates during strike window 06-May through 08-May-2026

RADIOCHEMICAL: CTBTO radionuclide monitoring network — no atmospheric xenon or iodine isotope release detected at any of 4 Iranian radionuclide monitoring stations during strike window or subsequent 48 hours

RECONNAISSANCE: ADS-B exchange data — 3 US DoD ISR orbits (no-flite transponders) logged within 50km radius of Natanz on 09-May-2026, consistent with direct overflight assessment — no imagery or data released
analysis: The State's 'decimation' claim fails on every physical sensor modality. The absence of a seismic or infrasound signal consistent with large conventional detonations at any of the four targeted coordinates is thermodynamically determinative: destroying centrifuge cascades at industrial scale requires hundreds of kilograms of high explosive. The seismic signature of a site receiving that level of ordnance load would register on CTBTO instruments at regional distances regardless of any political preference to withhold the data. The radionuclide monitoring results are equally falsifying — a intact nuclear program operating a chemical conversion facility that is 94% of thermal baseline, with no isotope release detected, is consistent with an unchanged operational status. The tent structures visible in post-strike Maxar imagery are not decommissioning infrastructure — they are the operational footprint of a program that is running. The three ISR overflights with no data release, set against a public claim of confirmed destruction, is the tell.
verdict: SIPHONED VERDICT: No physical modality — seismic, radionuclide, thermal, or visual — corroborates the 'decimation' claim; the Iranian nuclear program appears operationally intact across all independent sensing channels.
